content:
Hello, I compile with success the linux kernel (I'm new in the ecosystem and I need to make some nooby question sorry) I just used the configuration that the CI use by using the following command

cp .github/workflows/kernel-x86_64-debug.config .config
make LLVM=1 -j$(nproc)
# the make ask me a bouch confing where I press monkey enter!

My question is there i some tricky to make this first step? I can copy my os config instead if I would like to install the kernel in my machine?

In addition, there is any suggestion ro work around to avoid press random enter for specify the missing the make config variable? there is some make target that do this?

Sorry for the stupid question

In addition, there is any suggestion ro work around to avoid press random enter for specify the missing the make config variable? there is some make target that do this?

make LLVM=1 olddefconfig should work for that.

My question is there i some tricky to make this first step? I can copy my os config instead if I would like to install the kernel in my machine?

Not sure what you meant by this (was it answered with the olddefconfig part?).

Sorry for the stupid question

Nobody is born knowing Linux Makefile targets!
This topic was moved here from #General > Compiling Trick 101 by Miguel Ojeda.

Miguel Ojeda said:

In addition, there is any suggestion ro work around to avoid press random enter for specify the missing the make config variable? there is some make target that do this?

make LLVM=1 olddefconfig should work for that.

Thanks this works :)
